Forest Loop  
 


Slot: EAR
AC: 2
DEX: +3 WIS: +3 AGI: +3
WT: 0.1 Size: TINY
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Forest Loop

Slot: EAR
AC: 2
DEX: +3 WIS: +3 AGI: +3
WT: 0.1 Size: TINY
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Item Type:Jewelry
Stackable:No
Merchant Value:300 pp 0 gp 0 sp 0 cp
Tribute:300
Lucy Entry By:Tiana
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-12-16 19:02:14
Page Updated:Mon Jan 23rd, 2023

Expansion: Ruins of Kunark
Average Price: 292pp Pricing Data...
Rarity: Very Rare
Level to Attain: 20
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Burning Woods
NPC Name
a forest giant ancient
a forest giant arbor
a forest giant verdant
Gorgul Paclock
The Fabled Gorgul Paclock

Dreadlands
NPC Name
a mountain giant brae
a mountain giant craig
a mountain giant patriarch
a mountain giant steep
a ravishing drolvarg
Klaldran the Bald

Frontier Mountains
NPC Name
a mountain giant brae
a mountain giant hillock
a mountain giant patriarch
a mountain giant tump
mountain giant prospector

Warsliks Woods
NPC Name
a forest giant
a forest giant evergreen
a forest giant greenwood
a forest giant sapling


Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Burning Woods
Dreadlands
Frontier Mountains
Warsliks Woods

they still drop, the problem is that it's a less than 1% chance per magelo's tracking: https://eq.magelo.com/item/11538/Forest-Loop

Forest Loop

5 posts
Didn't know they were that rare - thanks for the info.

# Jan 07 2012 at 1:20 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592792637227
14 posts
I have killed tons of these, I'm on vulak now and I have killed around 100 just got one to drop off a Greenwood. Not sure that I have ever looted one before. I have seen them drop in groups in fm, but never won roll.

Good luck they still drop just not often.
▲ Page top
Loop
QuoteReply
# Feb 26 2012 at 3:11 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1642983438479
Scholar
263 posts
On Fippy: I don't farm the earrings specifially, but I do use the WW giants for exp from 21-24ish. I've noticed the loot is very random. Last night i got 2 earrings in about 4 hours with a mix of low grade gems and runes. A few nights ago, i got several leg choppers and one axe, time before that was hammers of flattening. Over all, i seem to get a couple loot items of a random nature during the night providing a nice $$ bump on a new (to me) server.

# Jan 02 2006 at 5:55 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591848061553
View User Forum
122 posts
Well, Well, just got one of these from the giant fort in Warslik's Wood, didn't even realize I got it, because well I was a tad displeasured at not finding any jade shards, so I tore apart the whole camp in a fit of Dwarven angst, I submitted a screenshot of it also :). Was at the vendor selling stuff and I clicked on it and the vendor said 274p and I was like :O !!!! Looked at stats and said oh yeah Im wearing this. Almost sold it to the vendor without even looking... sad sad. Nice earring though.
▲ Page top
profit value
QuoteReply
# Feb 17 2005 at 1:02 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591786716871
10 posts
i bought a forest loop about a year ago for 50pp.....just sold it to a vendor for 285pp....not a bad investment if ya can get em...
▲ Page top
Loops
QuoteReply
# Sep 25 2004 at 7:56 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593966081797
View User Forum
Scholar
366 posts
I spent several levels with my magician in Dreadlands, killing all the common mobs including giants, without ever finding a Forest Loop. In fact, I had pretty much forgotten that it was a possible drop. Then this morning, I killed a MG steep, and there was this earring - a Forest Loop. Great! Was killing a chicken when a MG craig blindsided me. I finished the cockatiel, then I killed the giant, and - lo and behold! - my second-ever Forest Loop. Never saw one, then got two back-to-back. :) I passed them on to my druid, since my mage really couldn't use them. But at least I know they still drop and can be found. It's just that, sometimes, you have to wait a long time.
▲ Page top
FLoop?
QuoteReply
# Jun 17 2004 at 3:03 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591801093973
3 posts
I have been reading and reading about these late 20's early 30's just romping around and whumping on stuff. Maybe i don't know the trick, but at 35, a bard of my stature has to single pull to the OT zone and medding after each one. what am I missing. I tried to run the Giant camp in Warslik's but got bored after the xp stopped and in three solo levels there I saw no FLoop!
▲ Page top
RE: FLoop?
QuoteReply
# Jun 04 2005 at 3:57 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591771858372
19 posts
Oh my good god! At 35 you should be soloing the ENTIRE giant fort in Frontier Mountains, or soloing 3-4 hill giants in Rathe Mountains. To find out what you're doing wrong, head over to eqdiva.com and read up on charm/swarm kiting.

I've been swarm kiting giants in Frontier Mountains from level 28, I can safely pull 10-15 giants although 4-5 is ideal, and I've seen two Forest Loops so far.

Myxiplx of Erollisi Marr
▲ Page top
RE: FLoop?
QuoteReply
# Jun 04 2005 at 7:23 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
And.....if he's NOT a bard?

Also, notice this post was last year. A year's worth of mudflation makes mobs much easier.

Edited, Sat Jun 4 08:26:14 2005
▲ Page top
RE: FLoop?
QuoteReply
# Jun 17 2004 at 4:03 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
At your level, you should be at the FM camp, not the Warsliks one. Alternately, and with a good group, you could fight in DL, although if you do that, hunt the roamers near KC N wall, not the fort, as you get fewer adds,and at 35, a couple adds could mess you up.
▲ Page top
